title: Identity Proofing Evidence for HR Onboarding Events

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 IA-12 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 IA-12 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.33: Identity Proofing Evidence for HR Onboarding Events.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.33' running on snow:sc_req_item onboarding catalog, or HEC JSON id_proof_status without government ID numbers, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `snow:sc_req_item` onboarding catalog, or HEC JSON `id_proof_status` without government ID numbers
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC)
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item earliest=-90d short_description="*Onboard*"
| eval proof_complete=if(state="Closed" AND match(short_description,"(?i)id proof"),1,0)
| stats latest(_time) as closed_time, values(state) as states by number, opened_at
| where proof_complete=0 AND relative_time(now(),opened_at) < 2592000
description: Tracks completion timestamps of identity proofing checkpoints from HR systems for IA-12 identity evidence (process control, not PII in Splunk).
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:sc_req_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Never index raw ID document numbers — use status flags only; (2) align `short_description` filters with catalog; (3) alert HR on overdue proofing; (4) map retention to AU-11. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item`.
visualization: Table (open onboarding), Time chart (cycle time), Single value (SLA breaches). Key fields: `closed_time`, `number`, `opened_at`, `proof_complete`, `states`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:sc_req_item` onboarding catalog, or HEC JSON `id_proof_status` without government ID numbers.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Never index raw ID document numbers — use status flags only; (2) align `short_description` filters with catalog; (3) alert HR on overdue proofing; (4) map retention to AU-11.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item earliest=-90d short_description="*Onboard*"
| eval proof_complete=if(state="Closed" AND match(short_description,"(?i)id proof"),1,0)
| stats latest(_time) as closed_time, values(state) as states by number, opened_at
| where proof_complete=0 AND relative_time(now(),opened_at) < 2592000
```

Understanding this SPL

**Identity Proofing Evidence for HR Onboarding Events (IA-12)** — Tracks completion timestamps of identity proofing checkpoints from HR systems for IA-12 identity evidence (process control, not PII in Splunk).

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:sc_req_item` onboarding catalog, or HEC JSON `id_proof_status` without government ID numbers. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:sc_req_item. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **proof_complete** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by number, opened_at** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where proof_complete=0 AND relative_time(now(),opened_at) < 2592000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open onboarding), Time chart (cycle time), Single value (SLA breaches)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for tracks completion timestamps of identity proofing checkpoints from hr systems for identity evidence (process control, not pii in splunk). This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
